briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. Welcome to the climate briefing for April 26, 2026. Today, we will provide a comprehensive overview of the positive developments in carbon neutrality efforts and renewable energy transition, both domestically and internationally. We will also address the realistic threats posed by climate change and the diverse responses from the global community.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Expansion of Carbon Neutrality Practices and Strengthening of Education
sections.content: Domestically, community efforts are ongoing, with Changnyeong-gun launching a carbon neutrality campaign in conjunction with the 18th Climate Change Week [1]. Daegu City, in particular, is encouraging citizen participation by recruiting the '3rd Youth Supporters' to spearhead carbon neutrality initiatives [4, 7, 11, 14]. Gwangmyeong City and Uiseong-gun are also expanding lifestyle-based carbon neutrality practices, moving beyond ESG solidarity and the '10-minute lights-out' campaign [6, 9]. Changes are also evident in the corporate and educational sectors: iM Bank is contributing to greenhouse gas reduction by establishing 'circular exchange offices' [5]; Boryeong Nampo Middle School is conducting bicycle tours for carbon neutrality; and the Halla Women's New Job Center is integrating carbon neutrality values into its vocational training programs [10, 12]. Additionally, Korea and the UNFCCC have extended their greenhouse gas education cooperation for another five years, reinforcing efforts to build capacity for future generations [2].

sections.title: ⚠️ Realistic Threats and Economic Impacts of Climate Change
sections.content: Climate change poses severe threats both domestically and internationally. In Korea, apple farmers are experiencing difficulties due to high oil prices and abnormal weather patterns, underscoring that the climate crisis is a national challenge impacting all sectors of society [3, 8]. The extension of Busan's Haeundae Beach operating period until September 15 due to prolonged heat can also be attributed to the direct impact of unusual weather [20]. Internationally, warnings have been issued that California is sacrificing its economic future at the altar of climate change [23], and reports indicate that climate change threatens the economic foundations of the Pacific region [27]. Moreover, research linking climate change to health inequality and supply chain disruptions, and its potential to affect the reproductive decline of species worldwide, further emphasizes the gravity of the situation [26, 30].

sections.title: 🌱 Progress in Renewable Energy Transition and Eco-friendly Mobility
sections.content: Progress in renewable energy transition and the development of eco-friendly technologies to address the climate crisis is steadily advancing. Domestically, the construction of renewable energy-based AI data centers is gaining momentum. The news that microgrids are increasingly opting for battery storage in an era where renewable energy accounts for 46% of power generation underscores the critical importance of energy efficiency [15, 16]. Imsil-gun is actively supporting its community's renewable energy transition by subsidizing up to 80% of the installation costs for new and renewable energy facilities, including solar and geothermal power [17, 18]. Meanwhile, at the motor show held in Beijing, China, various electric vehicle models from manufacturers like Hyundai and Geely Auto were unveiled, showcasing the future of eco-friendly mobility [19].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Response and Challenges
sections.content: The international community faces complex responses and challenges concerning climate change. Amid reports that the U.S. has shifted its stance on climate change, a climate change conference is underway in Colombia, fostering ongoing international discussions [22, 25]. Wildfires in Georgia have demonstrated a link between climate change and an increase in such events, underscoring the severity of these disasters [21]. Furthermore, new research suggesting that the extinction of Neanderthals was related to social isolation rather than climate change offers an intriguing perspective on the historical relationship between climate and humanity [28]. Corporations are also under increasing pressure to address climate change; China Carbon Neutral Development has lowered its acquisition value, and NatWest Bank is facing a confrontation at its general meeting over 'climate retreat' issues, thereby emphasizing corporate accountability [29, 31].

briefing.closing: In today's briefing, we have explored the promising aspects of carbon neutrality efforts and renewable energy transition, both domestically and internationally, alongside the various threats posed by climate change and the complex responses from the global community. Our collective efforts to overcome the climate crisis must continue. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: Haeundae Beach to Operate Until September Amidst Persistent Heat
articles.original_title: ‘땀 쏟아지는’ 9월까지 해운대해수욕장 가요
articles.description: Extended operation continues until September, following last year, due to persistent late heatwaves. As the trend of unyielding late summer heat is projected to continue until September this year, Busan's Haeundae Beach will extend its operating period until September, similar to last year. Haeundae District announced on the 26th that Haeundae Beach opened on June 26th and will operate until September 15th. Songjeong Beach is scheduled to open on the same day and operate until August 31st. Haeundae District cited the climate change trend of persistent late heat...

summary.phenomenon: Busan's Haeundae Beach will extend its operating period until September 15th this year, following last year's extension, due to the persistent late heatwave. Haeundae Beach opened on June 26th, and Songjeong Beach is scheduled to open on the same day and operate until August 31st.
summary.cause: The operating period of the beach has been extended due to the climate change trend of persistent late heatwaves.
summary.outlook: The trend of a persistent late heatwave, with temperatures not subsiding, is expected to continue until September this year.

summary.phenomenon: A new study indicates that the extinction of Neanderthals is linked to social isolation.
summary.cause: A new study proposes that the extinction of Neanderthals is attributable to social isolation rather than climate change.

articles.original_title: Toxins plus climate harms likely cause of reduced fertility, study finds
articles.description: Researchers find ‘alarming’ effect on fertility across global species from simultaneous exposuresSimultaneous exposure to toxic chemicals and climate change’s impacts likely generates an additive or synergistic effect that increases reproductive harm, and may contribute to the broad global drop in fertility, new peer-reviewed research finds.The review of scientific literature considers how endocrine-disrupting chemicals, often found in plastic, coupled with climate change’s effects, such as heat

summary.phenomenon: Researchers have identified an 'alarming' impact on fertility across global species, potentially contributing to a widespread decline in global fertility rates.
summary.cause: Simultaneous exposure to toxic chemicals, such as endocrine-disrupting substances found in plastics, and the effects of climate change, including heat, is likely to generate an additive or synergistic effect that exacerbates reproductive harm.
summary.outlook: This combined exposure could contribute to a global reduction in fertility.

articles.title: 냇웨스트, '기후 후퇴' 문제로 주주총회 대결 직면
articles.original_title: NatWest faces AGM showdown over ‘climate backtracking’
articles.description: Shareholders including the Church of England back call for protest votes against bank’s chairNatWest is at risk of an embarrassing showdown at its shareholder meeting this week as investors and scientists call for an urgent reversal of what they describe as “climate backtracking”.Campaigners including ShareAction are calling for protest votes against the bank’s chair, Rick Haythornthwaite, at its annual meeting in Edinburgh on Tuesday. Continue reading...

summary.phenomenon: NatWest Bank is facing a confrontation with shareholders and scientists at its Annual General Meeting in Edinburgh this week over 'climate backtracking'. Shareholders, including the Church of England, are supporting protest votes against the bank's chair, Rick Haythornthwaite.
